title: narcissus × hannibalis nothosubsp. pacensis
intro: Narcissus × hannibalis nothosubsp. pacensis, also known as the Pacific narcissus or Pacific daffodil, is a nothosubspecies of flowering plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, often referred to as a natural hybrid of Narcissus pseudonarcissus and Narcissus obsoletus. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, and its bulbs contain alkaloids with potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Narcissus × hannibalis nothosubsp. pacensis may include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial effects, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the bulbs are typically dried, powdered, or tinctured, and used as a natural remedy for various ailments, although caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity and side effects.
